analysis: Seven nights of data, and the night of the 13th into the 14th is the one that needs talking about. You were awake for nearly two and a half hours from around 2:30am, which tanked the whole thing — your total sleep came in well under where it needs to be, with only about 16 minutes of deep sleep and your REM scattered across the early part of the night before the long wake swallowed everything. The wins are real though: that REM was stacking nicely in the first half, and your deep sleep hit fast, which means your wind-down routine is doing something. One rough night in seven isn't a pattern, it's just a night — the stack you've built around bedtime is clearly working when your nervous system cooperates.
